root_text.sn47.51-62:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gaṅgā nadī pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā;
root_text.sn47.51-62:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bhāvento cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro.
root_text.sn47.51-62:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bhāvento cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro?
root_text.sn47.51-62:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.51-62:2.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.51-62:2.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bhāvento cattāro satipaṭṭhāne bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro”ti vitthāretabbaṁ.

translation_text.sn47.51-62:0.3: Twelve Discourses on the Ganges River, Etc.
translation_text.sn47.51-62:1.1: “Mendicants, the Ganges river slants, slopes, and inclines to the east.
translation_text.sn47.51-62:1.2: In the same way, a mendicant who develops and cultivates the four kinds of mindfulness meditation slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn47.51-62:2.1: And how does a mendicant who develops the four kinds of mindfulness meditation slant, slope, and incline to extinguishment?
translation_text.sn47.51-62:2.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.51-62:2.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.51-62:2.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.51-62:2.6: That’s how a mendicant who develops and cultivates the four kinds of mindfulness meditation slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn47.51-62:3.1: Six on slanting to the east,
translation_text.sn47.51-62:3.2: and six on slanting to the ocean;
translation_text.sn47.51-62:3.4: and that’s how this chapter is recited.
